title: gmelina philippensis f. viridibracteata
intro: Gmelina philippensis f. viridibracteata, also known as the Green-Bracted Gmelina, is a plant species belonging to the family Verbenaceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which have been attributed to its bioactive compounds such as flavonoids and terpenoids. The plant has been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, and has been reported to have potential health benefits in reducing inflammation and improving immune function. In traditional medicine, Gmelina philippensis f. viridibracteata is commonly prepared as a tea, decoction, or tincture, and has also been used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
